cases.title: What is a Landlocked Country?
cases.description: A country that has no direct access to the sea/ocean.
steps: Definition: A country surrounded by land on all sides.
There are 16 landlocked countries in Africa.
Examples: Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, South Sudan, Zambia, Malawi.
Problem: Imports and exports are expensive because they must pass through other countries.

cases.title: Gateway Countries
cases.description: Neighbors with sea ports that help landlocked countries.
steps: They have coastlines on the Indian Ocean.
Dar es Salaam (Tanzania) is another key port for Rwanda, Burundi, and Zambia.

cases.title: The Route to the Sea
cases.description: How goods travel from Kampala to the Ocean.
steps: The dashed line shows the route from Kampala to Mombasa.
Solution to problems: Joining regional groups like the EAC (East African Community) helps lower taxes and speed up transport.
